verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family (sperm-oocyte fusion proteins). The NCBI resolver identifies it as nuclear-resident. The canonical IZUMO proteins (IZUMO1, IZUMO2/SPARM1, IZUMO3) are testis-enriched or germline-restricted single-pass TM proteins essential for sperm-egg fusion; however, IZUMO4 is explicitly annotated as nuclear, not membrane-anchored. No documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death surface display, or activation-induced translocation. No evidence of TM domain, GPI anchor, or stable surface-partner tethering. No documented PM minority pool or dual localization. Not a secreted protein; not pMHC-only-intracellular. The nuclear localization and absence of membrane-anchoring topology classify it as strictly intracellular.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO family members are canonically germline/testis TM proteins; IZUMO4's nuclear assignment is surprising within that family. Confirm that IZUMO4 lacks a TM domain and is not a germline-restricted surface protein misclassified by the NCBI nuclear call.
